</user_query>

Exploring the bulk import screen and CSV format so we can add a template download link.

input.query: Where is CSV bulk import validated or parsed?

content.text:
Adding a download link using a single template constant (matching the backend headers) so we keep one source of truth in the import page.

[REDACTED]

input.old_string:
import type { CsvImportRowResult } from "@/types";

const schema = z.object({

input.new_string: Download template

content.text:
Here’s what changed: ### Bulk import template download 1. **`SESSIONS_IMPORT_CSV_TEMPLATE`** — One constant for the header row (same columns the API expects). 2. **Default textarea** — Uses that constant so the UI default always matches the downloadable file. 3. **“Download template”** — Link beside the CSV label; uses a `data:` URL with `download="wellspring-sessions-import-template.csv"` so clicking saves a CSV with the correct headers (no extra `public/` asset). ```11:18:frontend/src/app/(dashboard)/import/page.tsx /** Header row for session CSV import; must stay aligned with backend import validation. */ const SESSIONS_IMPORT_CSV_TEMPLATE = "client_row_id,program_id,title,duration_seconds,instructor_name,tags,position\n"; const schema = z.object({ ``` ```81:97:frontend/src/app/(dashboard)/import/page.tsx
Download template
``` Frontend tests pass. If you want a sample data row in the file as well, say so and we can extend the constant safely (keeping import validation in mind). [REDACTED]
